objective: Adopt Shirube discipline for Wasurezu development in single-agent minimal audit mode.
scope: Record producer, gate, and review sections separately.
Require standard audit for recovery, memory, MCP, host-runtime, redaction, provenance, token, migration, or data-model behavior changes.
non_goals: No merge-authority enforcement in the first adoption PR.

data_migration_impact.notes: Adoption scaffolding does not change Wasurezu data models.

recovery_behavior_impact.notes: First adoption PR documents recovery continuity requirements without changing recovery behavior.

host_runtime_impact.notes: No host invocation or restart behavior change in the first adoption PR.

redaction_provenance_impact.notes: Reports must identify source/provenance handling and avoid projecting secrets or private reasoning.

context_pack_non_applicability: No Kodama context-pack is required for the first Wasurezu adoption scaffolding PR.
tests_required: project-specific focused tests for changed recovery, memory, MCP, host, redaction, provenance, token, migration, or data-model behavior
rollback_plan: Revert docs/scaffolding PR for first adoption step.
For later behavior changes, restore previous schema or migration state using the documented rollback procedure.

forbidden: recovery correctness claim without deterministic evidence

non_claims: This Work Order does not grant merge authority.
This Work Order does not grant phase transition authority.
This Work Order does not make Wasurezu recovery behavior correct by itself.
This Work Order does not enable full runner automation, state_daemon automation, or AUN auto-runner operation.
promotion_criteria: Wasurezu dogfoods the profile on adoption scaffolding.
Standard audit covers at least one behavior-changing recovery or MCP task.
Missing Wasurezu Work Order field warnings are resolved in real usage.
A later Shirube PR receives review approval to promote dispatch gaps from WARN to BLOCK.
